title: Swimming with the Undertow | Dimensional
gist: Using a beach-undertow analogy, Dimensional argues that fighting markets by trying to time or outguess prices is a losing proposition, while riding along with market returns has historically built wealth, illustrated by the Russell 3000 Index over 2001-2025.

insights.claim: Trying to time markets or outguess their prices is often a losing proposition, whereas riding along with market returns has allowed investors to build wealth over time.
insights.speaker: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Reinforces a buy-and-hold, low-turnover posture over tactical timing; supports staying invested through volatility rather than trading around it.
insights.quote: Fighting against the current of markets by trying to time them or outguess their prices is often a losing proposition.
insights.locator: Anyone who has spent time in the ocean

insights.claim: Just as the best response to being caught in an ocean undertow is to swim with the current back to shore rather than fight it, the best investing response to market currents is to ride along with them rather than resist.
insights.speaker: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: A mental model for coaching clients to stay disciplined and avoid reactive selling during drawdowns.
insights.quote: If you’re caught in an unexpected current, the best course of action is to swim with it as you make your way back to the shore.
insights.locator: Anyone who has spent time in the ocean

insights.claim: A US total-market equity benchmark, the Russell 3000 Index, is cited over the 2001-2025 period as evidence that harnessing market returns builds wealth over time.
insights.speaker: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
insights.so_what: Anchors the stay-invested argument to a broad-market benchmark spanning multiple full market cycles, including the 2008 crash and 2020 shock.

source_meta.author: Wes Crill, PhD, Senior Client Solutions Director and Vice President
